rationale: The signal set is heavily weighted toward Google autocomplete searches (95 items) showing consistent high-volume interest in morphs, breeding, and specific morphs like Cappuccino, Lilly White, and Axanthic. YouTube videos reinforce strong demand for breeding advice, morph identification, and "warning" content about risky pairings (e.g., Super Cappuccino breeding risks, pairings to avoid). However, most Google Trends, Bluesky, and forum data are absent or low-engagement. The autocomplete data confirms demand but is generic. Two topics already queued (lilly-white-identification-breeding, axanthic-breeding-strategy) eliminate those morphs. YouTube's "WARNING, NEVER BREED THESE CRESTED GECKOS TOGETHER" (3.4k views, 2 months) and "Don't buy super cappuccino... it's a scam" (2k views, 1 year) suggest real breeding confusion and misconceptions worth correcting. The Cappuccino topic is published but the data hints at audience frustration ("Why So Much Hate") and breeding risk narratives. I'll focus on one high-signal topic that avoids published slugs and already-queued topics: a breeding pairing misconception angle with actionable genetics.
